description: Machine source-of-truth for project structure. Every agent/dev reads this before working; update the module's entry when its status changes. Size metrics under `metrics` are regenerated by scripts/generate_catalog.py and must not be hand-edited.

status_legend.enforced: Runtime code exists AND is covered by tests that block on violation.
status_legend.partial: Some runtime code exists but the intended capability/chain is incomplete.
status_legend.contract-only: Only an interface, schema, or policy YAML exists; no runtime behaviour.
status_legend.stub: Only README and/or empty __init__.py; no code and no contract.
status_legend.empty: Directory reserved by the frozen blueprint; nothing implemented yet.

metrics.note: Regenerated by scripts/generate_catalog.py --write. Do not hand-edit.

modules.purpose: Runtime enforcement of the CVF application profile: reads the profile YAML and exposes all 12 required_controls as callable gates.
modules.enforcement: policy_loader.py loads profile; gates in identity/permission/domain_lock/data_scope/risk/approval/evidence/audit/budget(cost)/termination/errors(refusal). cost+termination run and are tested now, load-bearing when an AI mode beyond NO_AI is enabled.
modules.next_step: Wire ai-gateway/ai-providers to call data_scope/budget/termination when an AI mode is enabled. Replace known-principals.yaml registry check with real authentication when P2-B is implemented.

modules.purpose: Declarative CVF profile for this application: risk classes, approval, evidence, domain lock, data, cost, refusal, termination, freeze policies. Does not copy CVF core.
modules.enforcement: Policy source only; all 12 controls now enforced by cvf-runtime (cost/termination AI-gated).
modules.contract: profile.yaml, risk-classes.yaml, approval-policy.yaml, evidence-policy.yaml, domain-lock.yaml, data-policy.yaml, cost-policy.yaml, refusal-policy.yaml, termination-policy.yaml, freeze-policy.yaml, provider-policy.yaml
modules.next_step: Keep policy YAML authoritative; changes here flow into cvf-runtime gates automatically.

modules.purpose: FastAPI backend: shifts, messages, operational events, corrections, tasks. Hosts three CVF golden verticals: event confirmation, post-freeze correction, and task create/transition.
modules.enforcement: events/router.py + services.py run the confirm chain; corrections/router.py + correction_service.py run the correction chain; tasks/router.py + task_service.py run task create/transition; shifts/router.py + shift_service.py run close/freeze (freeze requires identity/permission/shift_closed + an explicit audited override for the two prerequisites with no model yet). All reuse cvf-runtime gates and depend on the operations-ledger Ledger Protocol (backend via DATABASE_URL). domain/lifecycle.py enforces data-state and task-status transitions. 2026-07-22 (P-FIX-1): both InMemoryLedger and SqlLedger now block add_event/put_event/add_task/put_task when the parent shift is FROZEN (previously only InMemoryLedger blocked new records, and SqlLedger blocked nothing); CorrectionService uses allow_when_frozen=True as the sole permitted post-freeze mutation path.
modules.next_step: P-FIX-4: add the missing tasks.version column to migration 002 and tighten schema-parity checking. Only after the full P-FIX tranche: replicate the chain to remaining domains (customer requests, incidents, handovers).

modules.purpose: Channel Integration Edge: webhook gateway with signature verification, dedup, raw-payload preservation before any business system sees external input.
modules.enforcement: webhook/router.py verifies HMAC (constant-time) and fails closed on missing secret outside development; deduplication/store.py drops duplicates.
modules.next_step: Implement raw_payload, quarantine, rate_limit, routing, outbound modules (currently stub).

modules.purpose: Background jobs: message/event extraction, report generation, notification and outbound delivery, maintenance, scheduling, retry.
modules.enforcement: main.py + retry/policy.py present; all job modules are stub.
modules.next_step: Implement job modules once their owning packages exist.

modules.purpose: Mobile PWA + Desktop Web operational UI (React/Vite). Minimal shell today.
modules.enforcement: App.tsx shell + api.ts client + offline/queue.ts skeleton; feature folders are stub README only.
modules.next_step: Build feature verticals matching backend chain, starting with events/approvals.

modules.purpose: Source-of-truth persistence. Defines the Ledger Protocol and an append-only, dual-backend SqlLedger (SQLAlchemy Core over the existing migration schema; generic Uuid/JSON types work against SQLite or PostgreSQL from the same table definitions). InMemoryLedger (in workspace-api) is the offline/test backend.
modules.enforcement: ledger.py defines the Protocol (including transaction() unit-of-work); sql_ledger.py implements append-only INSERT for corrections/audit and maps tables.py to migrations 001+002; every mutating method accepts an optional unit= connection so callers can chain writes into one real SQL transaction (P-FIX-2). tables.py uses SQLAlchemy generic Uuid + JSON.with_variant(JSONB, 'postgresql') so one schema definition serves both backends, WITH matching FK/CHECK/column-set/nullability verified against the migration (P-FIX-4: tightened test_schema_parity.py compares exact column names and nullability, not just table/FK-table presence; negative-tested by reproducing the tasks.version-missing bug). evidence_links table (P-FIX-3) persists OperationalEvent/Task evidence, written once at creation. make_engine() turns ON SQLite foreign-key enforcement (off by default) so SQLite and PostgreSQL enforce the same integrity. Selected at runtime by workspace-api ledger_factory via DATABASE_URL. Verified end-to-end against SQLite (round-trip across reconnect, FK/CHECK rejection, append-only, atomic rollback on audit failure, evidence round-trip). PostgreSQL round-trip has NEVER been run live in this environment (no Docker daemon) - the known static schema defect is fixed, but this is not the same as a verified live run.
modules.next_step: Run the same integration + integrity + evidence + parity suite against a real PostgreSQL instance (docker compose up postgres) once available - this is a pre-ship gate, not required for ordinary SQLite-based development. Map remaining migration tables (messages, customer_requests, reports) into tables.py as tranches need them.

modules.purpose: Domain language and invariants for shift, message, event, task, customer request, incident, handover, report, approval, correction, audit.
modules.enforcement: None yet. Domain models currently live inline in workspace-api/domain/models.py.
modules.next_step: Extract domain models out of workspace-api into this shared package.

modules.purpose: Boundary to CVF Refinery: normalize, terminology, dedupe, redact, classify, conflict detection, context candidates.
modules.enforcement: contracts/refinery_contract.yaml only; no runtime bridge.
modules.next_step: Implement the refinery boundary before any LLM context is built.

modules.purpose: Bridge to CVF policy evaluation, approval gates, refusal, evidence, audit and fallback.
modules.enforcement: policy-evaluation/policy_contract.yaml only. NOTE: cvf-runtime now provides the concrete in-workspace enforcement; reconcile scope with cvf-runtime.
modules.next_step: Decide whether this stays a contract or is superseded by cvf-runtime.

modules.purpose: Provider-neutral model routing, context control, budget, structured output, validation, fallback, kill switch.
modules.enforcement: contracts/provider_interface.py defines the LLM contract; no router implementation.
modules.next_step: Implement model router + budget/kill-switch when an AI mode beyond NO_AI is enabled.

modules.purpose: Shared interface for channel adapters: verify, parse, attachments, send, delivery status, health, credential refresh.
modules.enforcement: adapter-interface/adapter.py defines the interface; used by integration-edge.
modules.next_step: Provide concrete adapters in channel-adapters.

modules.purpose: Concrete adapters for internal PWA, customer portal, generic webhook, Zalo, WhatsApp, email, SMS, and mocks.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement generic-webhook + mock adapters first; Zalo/WhatsApp remain mock until credentials.

modules.purpose: Adapters for NO_AI, RULES_ONLY, OpenAI-compatible, non-compatible, local, enterprise, subscription, and mock providers.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement NO_AI + RULES_ONLY + mock providers first.

modules.purpose: In-app, push, email, SMS, outbound channels and escalation.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement after outbound channel adapters exist.

modules.purpose: Build report drafts from confirmed records, validate evidence, export PDF/Excel.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement once operations-ledger returns confirmed records.

modules.purpose: Map external identities to internal users/customer contacts with human confirmation.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement when external channels are integrated.

modules.purpose: Route messages to workspace, shift, vessel, customer, incident, or fallback.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Implement after operations-domain and identity-mapping.

modules.purpose: Canonical JSON Schemas that form the stable boundary between core, providers, channels, Refinery and CVF.
modules.enforcement: Schemas only.
modules.next_step: Keep schemas authoritative as domains are implemented.

modules.purpose: Identifiers, time, errors, result, validation, observability and security primitives.
modules.enforcement: None yet.
modules.next_step: Extract shared primitives as real code appears across packages.
